mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: An upward inflection in the index of industrial production reflects recovering manufacturing capacity and easing supply constraints, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns in >70% of observations, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 290 force central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Robust nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates resilient labor demand, supporting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent proxy effects in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above trend levels expands aggregate corporate revenues, directly translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window, or predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and manufacturing slowdowns, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to maintain statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.
